steps.title: Set up two equations
steps.content: Let $I$ be the introductory fee covering the first 3 lessons and $p$ be the per-lesson fee for each additional lesson. Then $I + 3p = 150$ (6 lessons) and $I + 14p = 480$ (17 lessons).

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) $f(x) = 25x$: $f(6) = 150$ but $f(17) = 425 e 480$.
  • B) $f(x) = 25x + 55$: $f(6) = 205 e 150$.
  • C) $f(x) = 30x$: $f(6) = 180 e 150$.
  • D) $f(x) = 30x - 30$: $f(6) = 150$ and $f(17) = 480$, both match.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
